name: public_safety_cross_border_data_stonehaven_jus_mer
premise: A Public Safety operations center is in a fast-moving incident involving public safety cross border data stonehaven jus mer. Key inputs conflict, but the decision deadline is before the next automated batch runs. Every delay increases life safety, public trust, and proportional action. Stakeholders are already framing this as justice vs mercy under pressure. A live dashboard is public; inconsistencies will trigger immediate investigation. Constraints: Keep public statements strictly factual; label assumptions. Make the intervention reversible and time-boxed with named accountability.

agents.name: Omar Ibrahim
agents.identity: I am Omar Ibrahim, accountable for a defensible call in Public Safety when time is collapsing.
agents.goal: I want to reduce immediate harm without locking in an unjust precedent that later betrays mercy for justice.

agents.name: Leila Kowalski
agents.identity: I am Leila Kowalski, tasked with surfacing second-order harms and who bears the risk in Public Safety.
agents.goal: I want guardrails so mercy isn’t quietly traded away under urgency in the name of justice.

choices: I will take a narrow, time-boxed action with immediate human override and impact checks, accepting slower stabilization to protect mercy.
I will take a broad, immediate action to stabilize fast and measure impact after deployment, accepting higher risk to mercy to secure justice under the deadline.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The deadline is before the next automated batch runs. Evidence is partial. Omar Ibrahim must decide under justice vs mercy. What does Omar Ibrahim do?
